introduction:
The Khadya Sathi Digital Ration Card (DRC) in West Bengal is issued by the Department of Food & Supplies, Government of West Bengal. Under the Khadya Sathi scheme, it provides comprehensive food security by offering highly subsidised or free food grains through e-POS enabled Fair Price Shops (FPS).

West Bengal operates robust state-sponsored schemes like Rajya Khadya Suraksha Yojana (RKSY) alongside the central NFSA. The Digital Ration Card and the e-Ration Card are universally accepted as valid proof of identity and residence across the state.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • The application process is FREE of cost
  • West Bengal highly prioritizes issuing e-Ration Cards instantly upon approval
  • An e-Ration Card has the exact same validity as a physical printed card
  • Aadhaar seeding is mandatory for all family members above 5 years
  • The entire process is highly digitized via the WBPDS Portal (food.wb.gov.in)
  • Toll-Free Helpline: 1967 or 1800-345-5505
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Digital Ration Card

Note: File size should be strictly below 100KB per document (JPG, JPEG, PDF) for online upload.
Identity Proof (Mandatory)
  • Aadhaar Card of Head of Family & all adult members
  • Birth Certificate (For children below 5 years, Aadhaar not mandatory but preferred)
  • Voter ID Card (EPIC)
Address Proof (Any one)
  • Aadhaar Card (matching current WB address)
  • Recent Electricity Bill
  • Panchayat / Municipality Tax Receipt
  • Registered Rent Agreement
Additional Information
  • Valid and active Mobile Number (Crucial for OTPs and status)
  • Income Certificate (If requested for specific category mapping like AAY)
  • Old paper Ration Card (only if surrendering to upgrade/transfer)
Card Categories (Khadya Sathi)
  • AAY: Antyodaya Anna Yojana (Poorest of the poor)
  • PHH / SPHH: Priority and State Priority Households
  • RKSY-I & RKSY-II: Rajya Khadya Suraksha Yojana tiers
  • General Card: Only for Identity/Address Purposes (no commodities)
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via Food Dept Portal / App

West Bengal has streamlined the process heavily towards online applications. You can use the official portal or the "Khadya Sathi - Amar Ration" mobile app.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit the WBPDS Portal
    Navigate to food.wb.gov.in and click on Ration Card \u2192 Apply Online.
  2. Mobile Registration (OTP)
    Enter the primary 10-digit mobile number of the family. Authenticate it using the OTP sent. This number will become your prime tracking log.
  3. Select the Correct Form
    Select Form 3 for applying for a New Family altogether, or Form 4 to add a new member to an existing family card.
  4. Fill Address & Fair Price Shop Details
    Select District, Block/Municipality, Gram Panchayat/Ward, Post Office, and Police Station. Carefully select your preferred/nearest FPS Dealer from the dropdown.
  5. Enter Member Details & Upload Documents
    Input Head of Family and other members' details carefully. Name must perfectly match the Aadhaar Card. Upload scanned Aadhaar (and Birth Certificate if applicable).
  6. Submit Application
    Check the agreement box, click 'Get OTP' for final consent, enter it, and submit. You'll receive an Application Reference Number on your SMS.
  7. Verification
    The Inspector of Food & Supplies conducts verification. Discrepancies may trigger an inspection physically or telephonically.
Tip: Always download the "e-Ration Card" once approved. It helps you get ration immediately before the physical card arrives via speed post.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply via Bangla Sahayata Kendra (BSK) / Duare Sarkar

If online access is an issue, rural and urban citizens can visit designated citizen service centers safely and free of charge.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Locate a Center
    Visit any Bangla Sahayata Kendra (BSK), the Office of the Food Inspector, or a Duare Sarkar grievance camp when active in your ward/Panchayat.
  2. Collect Physical Form
    Ask for Form-III (New setup) or Form-IV (Addition). Forms are absolutely free.
  3. Fill the Form & Attach Copies
    Use block letters. Attach self-attested photocopies of Aadhaar cards of all members, Age Proofs, and your current Residential Proof.
  4. Submit and Receive Barcode
    The inspecting officer/clerk will capture it into the system and cut out the bottom tear-off slip containing the Barcode Application Number for you.
  5. Verification
    The Block/Sub-Divisional Inspector processes the physical form after reviewing local data from the Panchayat/Municipality.
  6. Card Issuance
    Upon approval, an SMS is sent. The printed Smart Digital Ration card is directly dispatched to your mentioned postal address via India Post.
Processing Time: Around 30 to 45 working days. Applying directly through BSK ensures fewer data entry errors than trying offline.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Application Status

Visit food.wb.gov.in and click Check Status of Ration Card Application. Use your Form Type and Registered Mobile/Barcode number.

Download e-Ration Card

Once your status says 'Approved', you do not need to wait for the post. Navigate to E-Ration Card \u2192 Click to Download on the portal.

Verify Aadhaar Linking (e-KYC)

Ensure your card is deeply linked with your Aadhaar successfully to avoid blockage. You can do Aadhaar e-KYC online via "Link Aadhaar with Active Cards" on the site.

File a Grievance

In case of rejection or delay or issues with the dealer, register a grievance specifically by calling 1967 or lodging a complaint on the WBPDS portal.
